differential_diagnoses: Acute kidney injury (acute tubular necrosis vs. cortical necrosis)
next_steps: Remove the tourniquet and assess for reperfusion injury and local wound management
Administer appropriate polyvalent antivenom as soon as possible
Resuscitate with fluids and blood products (packed red blood cells, fresh frozen plasma, platelets) to stabilize hemodynamics and correct coagulopathy
Perform immediate obstetrical consultation for emergency delivery evaluation in the setting of life-threatening maternal hemorrhage and coagulopathy
Continuous fetal monitoring if available and monitor for signs of fetal distress
Initiate renal support or consult for potential dialysis if indicated for severe hyperkalemia or persistent uremic symptoms
Monitor vital signs and serial hemoglobin/hematocrit, coagulation profile, and renal function

differential_diagnoses: Snakebite-induced consumptive coagulopathy (SICC) with systemic envenomation
Placental abruption secondary to hemotoxic coagulopathy
Acute kidney injury (acute cortical necrosis vs acute tubular necrosis)
Compartment syndrome of the lower extremity
next_steps: Immediate removal of the tourniquet with careful monitoring for reperfusion injury
Administration of appropriate polyvalent antivenom as soon as possible
Resuscitation with blood products (packed red blood cells, fresh frozen plasma, platelets) and intravenous fluids
Emergent obstetric evaluation for delivery in the setting of life-threatening maternal hemorrhage
Placement of a Foley catheter for strict hourly monitoring of urine output
Urgent laboratory assessment of serum electrolytes (specifically potassium), fibrinogen, and LDH
Wound assessment and monitoring for secondary infection or compartment syndrome
